title: Concurrent execution of transactions and DDL operations may lead to data inconsistency
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

deploy tidb:

```
tiup playground v6.5.0 --host 0.0.0.0
```



schema and initial data:

```sql
drop table if exists table2;
create table table2 (pkId integer, pkAttr0 integer, col1 double(10, 2), col2 decimal(10, 2), primary key(pkAttr0) NONCLUSTERED) PARTITION BY HASH(pkAttr0)  PARTITIONS 5 ;
alter table table2 add index idx0(pkAttr0);
alter table table2 add index idx1(col1);
alter table table2 add index idx2(col2);

insert into table2 (pkId,pkAttr0,col1,col2) values ("0","5","11.0","12.0");
insert into table2 (pkId,pkAttr0,col1,col2) values ("1","12","13.0","14.0");
insert into table2 (pkId,pkAttr0,col1,col2) values ("2","18","15.0","16.0");
insert into table2 (pkId,pkAttr0,col1,col2) values ("3","25","17.0","18.0");
insert into table2 (pkId,pkAttr0,col1,col2) values ("4","33","19.0","20.0");
```



| T1 (session 0)                                 | session1                                               | session2                                               |
| :--------------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------------------------- |
| begin;                                         | alter table table2 modify col1 double(10, 2) not null; | alter table table2 modify col1 double(12, 3) not null; |
| UPDATE table2 SET col1 = 10 WHERE pkAttr0 = 5; | -- return ok                                           | -- return ok                                           |
| commit; -- ok                                  |                                                        |                                                        |
|                                                | alter table table2 drop index idx2;                    |                                                        |
|                                                | -- return ok                                           |                                                        |



### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

After all workloads:

```sql
mysql> select count(*) from table2;                                                       
+----------+
| count(*) |
+----------+
|        5 |
+----------+
1 row in set, 1 warning (0.01 sec)

mysql> select count(*) from table2 force index(idx1);
+----------+
| count(*) |
+----------+
|        5 |
+----------+
1 row in set, 1 warning (0.00 sec)
```



### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

After all workloads:

```sql
mysql> select count(*) from table2;                                                       
+----------+
| count(*) |
+----------+
|        5 |
+----------+
1 row in set, 1 warning (0.01 sec)

mysql> select count(*) from table2 force index(idx1);
+----------+
| count(*) |
+----------+
|        3 |
+----------+
1 row in set, 1 warning (0.00 sec)

mysql> select * from table2;
ERROR 1105 (HY000): [components/tidb_query_executors/src/table_scan_executor.rs:422]: Data is corrupted, missing data for NOT NULL column (offset = 2)

mysql> admin check table table2;
ERROR 1105 (HY000): Miss column
```



### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->

TiDB v6.5.0
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), value DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN description TEXT;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Test1', 100.00);
UPDATE test_table SET value = NULL WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Concurrent execution of transactions and DDL operations
